questionnaire.name: SmartUrbanity - Your Experience on the Move
questionnaire.title: 4. Your Experience On the Move

sections.title: Welcome to Your Experience on the Move
sections.description:
This SmartUrbanity questionnaire asks about how you feel and what you experience while travelling, including walking, cycling, public transport, car, motorcycle and route quality. Your answers help identify barriers and opportunities for better urban mobility. Participation is voluntary and anonymous.

https://www.smarturbanity.com/about

sections.title: Emotional experience
sections.description: The following questions refer to your typical journey (the trip you most commonly make in your daily life, such as travelling to work, university, school, shopping, or other regular activities).

questions.text: How would you describe your emotional state during a typical journey on a route?
Thinking about your typical journey, how does the time it actually takes compare to how long you expect it to take?
How would you describe your level of tiredness or fatigue after completing your typical journey?
If your typical journey makes you feel tired, what do you think is the main cause?

options.text: Physical effort (e.g. walking, cycling, carrying bags)
Mental stress (e.g. traffic, navigation, time pressure, feeling unsafe or unsecure)
Environmental discomfort (e.g. heat, noise, poor air quality)
Waiting and standing (e.g. at stops, in queues)
I usually do not feel tired after my trips

questions.text: How often do you feel anxious or overwhelmed during your typical journey?
What specifically makes you feel anxious or overwhelmed during your typical journey? (Select up to 3 options)

options.text: Aggressive or unsafe behavior from other road users (drivers, cyclists, pedestrians)
Noise, crowding, or other sensory overload
Time pressure or risk of being late
Feeling isolated or alone while traveling
Safety and security concerns (e.g., accidents, crime)
I don't usually feel anxious or overwhelmed

questions.text: Compared to other travel options available to you (e.g., walking, driving, public transport), how would you rate the overall effort required by your usual mode of travel?

options.text: My current mode is significantly easier and more relaxing than any alternative
This is a bit easier than other ways I could travel
Regardless of the mode the effort feels roughly equal
This takes a bit more out of me than other options would
Far more demanding than alternatives (physically or mentally)

questions.text: How does the level of 'liveliness' (the presence of other people, shops, and activity) on your route affect your journey?

options.text: Very positively, lively environments make me feel safer or more comfortable
Somewhat positively, lively environments improve my travel experience
Neutral, the level of activity does not affect my journey
Somewhat negatively, quiet or empty areas can make me feel uncomfortable
Very negatively, overly busy or crowded areas make the journey stressful or difficult to navigate
